what: A Claude Code subagent DEFINITION FILE (Markdown, `.claude/agents/implementer.md`) that bounds the coding agent to "single slice, single worktree." Per design.md:240-251 it configures a subagent whose Role is "Implements exactly one highest-priority `unproven` item per session in an isolated git worktree," with Key behaviors: (1) read `feature_list.json` to identify the single highest-priority `unproven` item; (2) create a dedicated git worktree via `git worktree add`; (3) target ≤15 minutes / ≤1 feature per session; (4) produce exactly one atomic commit with the requirement ID in the git trailer; (5) explicitly does NOT run verification (Verifier's exclusive domain). Permissions: write to i…

open_gaps.gap: The artifact does not exist. `.claude/agents/` contains only a 0-byte `.gitkeep`; there is no implementer.md file on disk, despite it being a Phase-0 spine component whose acceptance test (design.md:630) is 'Creates worktree; commits with requirement ID in trailer'. The entire component is specified-but-unbuilt; task 10.2 is unchecked.
open_gaps.fix: Author `.claude/agents/implementer.md` per the Claude Code agent spec, encoding the role, the four key behaviors, and the permission scope from design.md:240-251 and tasks.md:208-211. Until then no Phase-0 smoke (design.md:630) or end-to-end loop (design.md:100-112) can run.

open_gaps.gap: No settings.json binding for the subagent. The design's settings.json block (design.md:159-201) registers ONLY hooks; there is no `subagents`/agent-registration entry for implementer.md (or any of the four agents) anywhere in design.md or tasks.md. How the runtime discovers/loads/invokes the agent file is unspecified.
open_gaps.fix: Either document that Claude Code auto-discovers `.claude/agents/*.md` by convention (and cite the version) or add the explicit agent-registration block to the settings.json design and to task 9.1, and add a smoke test (like tests/smoke/test_hook_config.py) asserting the four agents are registered/discoverable.

open_gaps.gap: Retry budget owner is named but never wired into the agent definition. requirements.md:359 says the retry budget (DEFAULT 3) is OWNED by 'the implementer loop' with run_state.retry_count, but implementer.md's key behaviors (design.md:244-249) and task 10.2 (tasks.md:208-211) never mention retry_count, the retry cap, or the REQ-LOOP-003 HANDOFF — so the ownership is asserted in NFR prose with no mechanism in the component it is assigned to.
open_gaps.fix: Add a key-behavior to implementer.md and a bullet to task 10.2: 'increments run_state.retry_count on a failed slice; on retry_count >= 3 stop and HANDOFF (REQ-LOOP-003)'. Optionally add a Z3/property check that retry_count>=3 routes to HANDOFF.

open_gaps.gap: acting_agent vs actor_agent naming inconsistency in the role-separation guard. Property 24 / design.md:1068 and the Postgres evidence_records column (design.md:473) use `actor_agent` and state it is canonical 'everywhere (no acting_agent)', but the Property-24 test (tasks.md:655) asserts rejection of records where `acting_agent == "implementer.md"`. The test field name contradicts the declared canonical field name.
open_gaps.fix: Rename `acting_agent` to `actor_agent` in tasks.md:655 to match design.md:473/1068 (the declared canonical name).

open_gaps.gap: Property 24 (implementer-cannot-self-verify) — the single most important invariant binding the implementer to separation-of-duties — has NO machine check. Grep of formal_verification_merged.py for actor_agent / role.separation / self-verif returns nothing. The 'no Z3 check' is not acknowledged; the requirements.md Critical Invariants list (1-7, 8-10) does not include role separation, and design.md:1066-1070 cites no CHECK id.
open_gaps.fix: Either add a Z3 CHECK asserting (evidenceCreated ∧ actorIsImplementer) is UNSAT to formal_verification_merged.py and cite it under Property 24, or explicitly annotate Property 24 as 'runtime/test-verified, no Z3 check' the way Properties 28/29 are annotated (design.md:1110,1120).

open_gaps.gap: Token-budget unit mismatch touching the implementer's HANDOFF trigger. requirements.md:357 sets the cost/token budget = 1,000,000 TOKENS per Slice, but run_state.token_cost_usd is NUMERIC(10,4) in USD (design.md:488) and the Stop-hook budget check reads `token_cost_usd >= token_budget_usd` (design.md:704). Tokens and USD are different units with no documented conversion; the implementer-loop budget cap is therefore expressed in two incompatible units.
open_gaps.fix: Pick one unit. Either add a `token_budget` (integer tokens) column to run_state and have the budget check compare tokens, or convert the 1,000,000-token default to a USD figure and state the model price assumption. Reconcile requirements.md:357 with design.md:488,704.

open_gaps.gap: design.md/tasks.md coverage of this component is THIN relative to its criticality. The implementer is a Phase-0 spine component and the load-bearing 'one slice' enforcer, yet its entire specification is ~10 prose lines (design.md:240-251) plus a 4-line task (tasks.md:208-211). Missing: the exact agent-spec frontmatter (name, description, tools, model), the precise tool-allowlist that realizes the 'worktree only' permission, the handoff protocol to the Verifier, the failure/retry behavior, and any test that exercises the markdown definition. By contrast the hooks each get full code blocks.
open_gaps.fix: Expand design.md section '.claude/agents/implementer.md' with the concrete agent frontmatter and tool allowlist, and expand task 10.2 to include a smoke test analogous to tests/smoke/test_hook_config.py that parses the agent file and asserts its permission scope (no tests/, no CI, no cross-worktree).

open_gaps.gap: Permission scope 'write to implementation source in its assigned worktree only' has no enforcement mechanism in the implementer definition. The narrative permission (design.md:251) is not realized by any hook or tool-allowlist: nothing prevents the implementer from writing outside .worktrees/{item_id}. The PreToolUse artifact guard (design.md:214) protects tests/, schema, CI — but not 'other worktrees' or 'host FS outside this slice's worktree' (that confinement is described only as a sandbox-mount property at design.md:524, a separate Phase task 46).
open_gaps.fix: Either (a) realize the per-worktree write confinement via the sandbox mount (design.md:524, task 46) and cross-reference it from implementer.md, or (b) add a PreToolUse check that blocks writes whose path is outside the active slice's worktree. State which mechanism enforces design.md:251.

open_gaps.gap: Highest-priority selection is unspecified beyond 'priority field'. implementer.md must pick 'the single highest-priority unproven item' (design.md:245) and feature_list schema has `priority` (integer, 1=highest, design.md:336-339), but no rule defines tie-breaking among equal-priority items or how dependencies (the CoverageItem.dependencies array) constrain selection — an item could be highest-priority yet have unproven dependencies.
open_gaps.fix: Specify the selection predicate in implementer.md: 'lowest priority integer among unproven items whose dependencies are all proven; ties broken by item id order'. Cite the dependencies field (design.md:354) so dependency-respecting selection is explicit.

critic_findings.detail: REQ-SPEC-018 (Requirement 29) / Property 30 / CHECK-13a/13b — the Structured Omission Declaration gate — is entirely absent from the draft's ears_stories AND open_gaps, yet it bears directly and pointedly on implementer.md. REQ-SPEC-018.1 requires a non-null `omission_declaration` field on the output of 'a subagent (initializer, research, verifier)' — conspicuously naming three of the four Phase-0 subagents and OMITTING the implementer. REQ-SPEC-018.3 then says 'THE JSON schema for EACH subagent output type SHALL include omission_declaration as a required, non-nullable field,' and the SubagentStop omission guard (design.md:218) rejects (exit 2) any subagent result lacking it. This is a mater…

REQ-CTRL-001 (Requirement 32) Agent Code Kill-Switch is missing from the draft's dependencies/open_gaps. Task 57 says 'wire OpenFeature SDK into agent entry points' and REQ-CTRL-001.2 requires that 'the affected agent capability SHALL be disabled within the polling interval (≤30s) WITHOUT requiring a process restart.' The implementer is an agent entry point / kill-switchable capability, so implementer.md is in scope for kill-switch wiring — but neither the draft's dependencies list nor its open_gaps mention flagd/OpenFeature, the kill-switch, or that implementer.md must be instrumentable for capability-disable. This is an unbuilt wiring obligation on the component just like the settings.json…

The SubagentStop evidence-schema gate (REQ-VERIFY-005 / criterion 9.5, subagent_stop_hook.py) as it bears on the IMPLEMENTER's own return is not addressed. The draft correctly notes the implementer 'Does NOT emit Evidence_Records' and is gated by the PreToolUse plan/scope gates, but it never asks what happens at SubagentStop when the implementer subagent returns. subagent_stop_hook.py (tasks.md:177-183) 'locates evidence field(s)' and blocks on missing/empty fields; combined with the omission guard (REQ-SPEC-018) this is the hook that fires on the implementer→verifier handoff boundary. The draft lists SubagentStop only abstractly via Property 24 and never names REQ-VERIFY-005/9.5 as a gate t…

The draft's ears_stories entries for CHECK-6a and CHECK-6b assert these Z3 checks 'constrain the scope gate that bounds when the implementer may start a new slice' / 'releases the implementer to start the next slice,' implying implementer-specific machine coverage. Independent inspection of formal_verification_merged.py (lines 101-102, 284-299) shows CHECK-6a/6b operate over GENERIC abstract Booleans `priorItemUnproven` and `newSliceStarting` with zero reference to the implementer (grep for 'implementer' in the harness returns 0 hits). These checks pin the scope-sequencing RULE (REQ-EXEC-005), not the implementer component. The draft already correctly flags that Property 24 has no Z3 check; …

REQ-VERIFY-001 / criterion 9.1's 'verify each Slice across five layers' bears on the implementer indirectly but is mis-scoped in the draft. The draft cites the line-coverage layer (Property 23) but omits that the FIVE-layer verification (structural, semantic, behavioral, security, performance+accessibility per the 2026-06-15 reconciliation) all operate on 'each Slice' — i.e., on the implementer's atomic-commit output as the unit of verification. The draft's design.md citations still reflect the older framing; Property 23's own text (design.md:1058-1062) and REQ-VERIFY-002 are the only verification linkages captured, but the Slice (the implementer's product) is the subject of all five layers …

The draft's token/cost-budget gap (open_gaps item on unit mismatch) is correct but understates the OWNERSHIP problem. REQ-LOOP-001 (14.1) states the token/cost budget is enforced 'per Slice' and the Slice is the implementer's unit of work, yet — exactly as with the retry budget (which the draft DID flag as 'named but never wired into the agent definition') — neither implementer.md's key behaviors (design.md:244-249) nor task 10.2 (tasks.md:208-211) mention the per-slice token/cost budget or its REQ-LOOP-002 HANDOFF trigger. The draft flags retry_count's missing wiring but does not apply the identical finding to the token/cost budget owned by the same implementer loop, even though task 44 cen…

Glossary term 'Slice' is cited by the draft, but two adjacent load-bearing glossary/architecture obligations on the implementer are not: (1) the Sandbox & Worktree Isolation section (design.md:514-527, task 46 / REQ-17.4) explicitly states the worktree 'created by the Implementer via git worktree add is mounted in as the writable working tree' and that this is what realizes the per-worktree write confinement — the draft's gap #9 correctly says implementer.md's write-confinement has no enforcement mechanism, but it frames the sandbox cross-reference as merely 'suggested,' missing that design.md:527 ALREADY ties REQ-17.4 isolation to 'the one-slice/one-worktree discipline FROM the Implementer …

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL restrict each coding subagent session to exactly one highest-priority `unproven` item.
ears_needing_work.how: implementer.md key-behavior: 'Reads feature_list.json to identify the single highest-priority unproven item' (design.md:245); tasks.md:210 'read feature_list.json for highest-priority unproven item'.

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHILE a Slice is in progress, THE System SHALL isolate it in a dedicated git worktree to prevent cross-slice collisions.
ears_needing_work.how: implementer.md key-behavior 'Creates a dedicated git worktree (git worktree add)' (design.md:246); mechanism delegated to tools/worktree_manager.py (task 11.1).

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL target a per-slice size of ≤15 minutes / ≤1 feature and SHALL produce one atomic commit per completed Slice.
ears_needing_work.how: implementer.md key-behaviors 'Targets ≤15 minutes / ≤1 feature per session' and 'Produces exactly one atomic commit with the requirement ID in the git trailer' (design.md:247-248).

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHEN a commit is created, THE System SHALL require ≥1 referenced requirement ID in the commit trailer.
ears_needing_work.how: implementer.md 'Produces exactly one atomic commit with the requirement ID in the git trailer' (design.md:248); task 10.2 cites Requirement 6.2 (tasks.md:211); Property 10 pins it; enforced at write-time by tools/traceability_writer.assert_commit_has_req_id (task 29.1).

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL perform completion verification with the independent Verifier...; the implementer SHALL NOT verify its own output.
ears_needing_work.how: implementer.md 'Does NOT run verification — verification is the Verifier's exclusive domain' (design.md:249); Property 24 forbids Evidence_Record with actor_agent=='implementer.md'.

ears_needing_work.criterion: IF a Slice exhausts its retry budget (DEFAULT = 3 retries), THEN THE System SHALL fail gracefully and hand off to a human.
ears_needing_work.how: Retry budget OWNER is explicitly 'the implementer loop'; counter in run_state.retry_count; on exhaustion hands off. But implementer.md key-behaviors (design.md:244-249) DO NOT mention retry budget, retry_count, or HANDOFF — owner stated in NFR text only, not wired into the agent definition.

ears_needing_work.criterion: WHILE a control loop runs, THE System SHALL enforce a hard iteration cap (DEFAULT = 25 turns per Slice) and a token/cost budget per Slice.
ears_needing_work.how: Cap enforced via `claude -p --max-turns 25` (design.md:822) at the loop level, not inside implementer.md; the implementer is the per-slice loop subject.

ears_needing_work.criterion: THE System SHALL use Claude Code subagents and hooks as the inner planner/implementer/verifier orchestrator...
ears_needing_work.how: implementer.md is the 'implementer' leg; orchestration wiring is task 43 (tasks.md:953). The wiring that actually invokes the four agents is an un-detailed future task.

ears_needing_work.criterion: For any commit created by the implementer subagent, the trailer must contain ≥1 requirement ID matching [A-Z]+-[A-Z]+-[0-9]{3}.
ears_needing_work.how: Names the implementer subagent directly as the commit author; pinned to REQ-TRACE-002; tested via tools/traceability_writer.parse_commit_trailers (task 29.2).

ears_needing_work.req_id: Property 24 (Subagent Role Separation — Implementer Cannot Self-Verify)
ears_needing_work.criterion: For any verification action, actor_agent shall be verifier.md, never implementer.md; no Evidence_Record may be created by the agent that wrote the implementation. NOTE: no Z3 CHECK pins this; harness has no actor_agent/role-separation assertion; also acting_agent vs actor_agent naming inconsistency.
ears_needing_work.how: Directly names implementer.md as the forbidden actor; tasks.md:655 asserts any Evidence_Record with acting_agent=='implementer.md' is rejected.

ears_needing_work.criterion: A bounded unit of implementation work, targeting ≤15 minutes / ≤1 feature, implemented in a dedicated git worktree and resulting in one atomic commit.
ears_needing_work.how: This glossary term IS the implementer's unit of work; implementer.md key-behaviors restate all three bounds (size, worktree, atomic commit).

ears_needing_work.criterion: Implementer subagent: 'One-slice coder; creates worktree; produces one commit' — Test to pass: 'Creates worktree; commits with requirement ID in trailer'. NOTE: file absent, so this test cannot pass.
ears_needing_work.how: Defines the smoke acceptance for the agent; but no test file in the design/tasks actually exercises a markdown agent definition end-to-end (the agent is prose, not executable).
